ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and rolls — sweet bread with dried fruit (raisin bread analogue under composite bread category)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code for raisin bread; mapped to composite sweet bread category A000E as closest defensible analogue.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of raisin bread is one slice weighing approximately 35 g, providing around 98 kcal, 3.4 g protein, 1.4 g fat, and 18.2 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Raisin Bread — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Raisin Bread (cr_id F05030017). All eight reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced from HPB at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18069 (Bread, raisin, enriched) as HPB does not report these fields; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for raisin bread
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 75 (high) for raisin bread is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database and the Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table (Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 18.2) / 100 = 13.65, rounded to 13.7. Serving size used: 35 g slice providing 18.2 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 323 mg per 100 g — borderline moderate sodium content
hypertension.guidance: Limit raisin bread to one to two slices per sitting (35–70 g, providing 113–226 mg sodium) and avoid adding salted butter or high-sodium spreads; choose wholemeal raisin bread variants for higher potassium and fibre, which partially offset sodium's blood-pressure effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (75), high total carbohydrate (52 g/100 g), and estimated sugar ~14 g/100 g from raisins and added sugar create a significant glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Restrict raisin bread to one slice (35 g) per occasion, pair with a protein source such as a boiled egg or unsweetened nut butter to blunt the glycaemic response, and prefer wholemeal or seeded raisin bread with a lower GI; avoid adding jam, honey, or sweetened spreads.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate sodium (323 mg/100 g) and refined carbohydrate load; low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g) and meaningful dietary fibre (5.9 g/100 g) are partially favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raisin bread can be included in a heart-healthy diet in modest portions (one slice, 35 g) given its low saturated fat and useful fibre content, but sodium should be monitored across the full day's intake and wholemeal variants are preferred to maximise fibre and reduce refined carbohydrate load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raisin bread is a soft, lightly sweetened yeast-leavened loaf made from wheat flour, dried raisins, sugar, salt, yeast, and a small amount of fat. It is baked until golden and has a tender, slightly chewy crumb studded with plump raisins that provide natural sweetness and concentrated fruit sugars. The flavour is mildly sweet with a subtle caramel note from the raisins, and some recipes include cinnamon or mixed spice. Per 100 g, raisin bread provides approximately 279 kcal, 9.6 g protein, 4.1 g fat, 52 g carbohydrate, 5.9 g dietary fibre, 323 mg sodium, 249 mg calcium, and 3.95 mg iron. The glycaemic index is high (GI 75), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The fibre content (5.9 g/100 g) is meaningful and supports digestive health. Clinically, raisin bread is a moderate-to-high concern food for type 2 diabetes due to its high GI and total carbohydrate load; it is borderline for hypertension due to its sodium content of 323 mg/100 g; and it is moderate concern for cardiovascular disease given its sodium and refined carbohydrate profile, partially offset by low saturated fat and useful fibre. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or snack, typically toasted and served plain or with a light spread.
common_user_queries: How many calories in raisin bread?
Is raisin bread good for diabetics?
Is raisin bread high in sugar?
Can I eat raisin bread if I have high blood pressure?
Raisin bread vs white bread nutrition
How much fibre in raisin bread?
Is raisin bread suitable for heart patients?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, sugar, potassium, B-vitamins, and water estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18069 (Bread, raisin, enriched) as a close analogue. GI value of 75 confirmed via University of Sydney GI Database and Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.9 g/100 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.0 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8 g/100 g), sugar (14.0 g/100 g), potassium (220 mg/100 g), cholesterol (0.0 mg/100 g), thiamine (0.22 mg/100 g), riboflavin (0.18 mg/100 g), niacin (2.4 mg/100 g), and water (33.0 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18069 (Bread, raisin, enriched) as HPB does not report these fields. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — detailed micronutrient data for this specific raisin bread formulation are not fully reported in either HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy entry 18069 as of 2026. 3. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g/100 g (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy 18069). 4. Cholesterol is reported as 0.0 g/100 g (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy 18069 — raisin bread made without eggs or dairy fat). 5. GI of 75 is classified as high (≥70). Glycaemic load per 35 g serving = (75 × 18.2) / 100 = 13.7 (medium GL). 6. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. 7. FoodEx2 code A000E is used as the closest defensible analogue for sweet bread with dried fruit; no dedicated raisin bread code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy as of 2026.
